data.title: XPENG launches global OTA 5.8.0 update
data.description:
• XPENG Motors (NYSE:XPEV) on Monday said that starting August 25, it will roll out the global OTA upgrade of XOS 5.8.0.
  • With XOS 5.8.0, the third major OTA release of 2025 follows the widely acclaimed versions XOS 5.4.5 in January and XOS 5.6.0 in May.
  • Owners will receive real-time alerts via the XPENG app and can remotely activate or deactivate the mode.

data.title: Thoma Bravo nears $2B deal for Verint Systems - Bloomberg
data.description:
Thoma Bravo is close to a deal to acquire call center software maker Verint Systems (NASDAQ:VRNT) in a deal that would value the call center software maker at ~$2B including debt, Bloomberg reported Sunday.

An agreement could be announced as soon as Monday, but no final decision has been made, according to the report.

Verint Systems (NASDAQ:VRNT), whose shares have dropped 38% in the past year, rose 6% in Friday's trading, giving the company a market value of ~$1.2B.

Bloomberg reported several weeks ago that Thoma Bravo was negotiating the terms of a potential deal for Verint (VRNT).

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper near $18B takeover deal for JDE Peet’s - WSJ
data.description:
Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) is close to a ~$18B deal for European coffee company JDE Peet’s (OTCPK:JDEPF) (OTCPK:JDEPY), The Wall Street Journal reported Sunday.

The combined company plans to later separate its beverage and coffee units, according to the report, which would essentially unwind the 2018 merger that brought together Keurig and Dr Pepper.

Keurig Dr Pepper's (NASDAQ:KDP) has a market value of ~$50B, while JDE Peet’s (OTCPK:JDEPF) (OTCPK:JDEPY), based in Amsterdam, is valued at ~$15B.

Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) reported in the most recent quarter that its U.S. beverage sales rose nearly 11% Y/Y to $2.7B, but its coffee business, which includes Green Mountain coffee and Keurig machines, has long struggled, and its U.S. coffee sales were roughly flat in the quarter.

The two companies already share common ownership: European-based investment firm JAB Holding has majority control of voting power at JDE Peet's (OTCPK:JDEPF) (OTCPK:JDEPY) and owns a stake in Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) and was the driver behind the original Keurig-Dr Pepper merger.

data.title: Netflix ‘Kpop Demon Hunters’ leads weekend box office, dethroning 'Weapons'
data.description:
Kpop Demon Hunters, an animated musicial from Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX), took the top domestic box office this weekend, bringing in at least $18M.

That was enough to unseat Weapons, from Warner Bros. (NASDAQ:WBD), which slipped to second place after taking the top spot in its first two weekends of release. Weapons earned around $15.6M in North America.

Although Netflix is not reporting box office grosses for Kpop Demon Hunters, Variety estimated it earned $18M-$20M. 

The success of Kpop Demon Hunters, presented in theaters as a sing-along version for one weekend only, is unusual considering that the movie was released on the streaming service's platform on June 20.

However, since then, it has become Netflix's second most streamed movie of all time. Adding to the musical's appeal is that three of its original songs are trending in the Billboard music charts.

Rounding out the top 3 was Disney's (NYSE:DIS) Freakier Friday, bringing in $9.2M in its third weekend out.

Top 6 Films – Weekend of Aug. 22–24, 2025 (Domestic Estimates)

  1. Kpop Demon Hunters (NASDAQ:NFLX) - $18M | Cume: $18M

  2. Weapons ("WB") — $15.6M | Cume: $115.8M

  3. Freakier Friday (NYSE:DIS) — $9.2M | Cume: $70.5M

  4. The Fantastic Four: First Steps (DIS) — $5.9M | Cume: $257.3M

  5. The Bad Guys 2 (CMCSA) — $5.1M | Cume: $66.2M

  6. The Naked Gun (PAR) — $2.95M | Cume: $47.6M

Source: Comscore, Variety

data.title: Notable healthcare headlines for the week: Novo Nordisk, Medtronic, Novavax in focus
data.description:
Wall Street jumped sharply on Friday, with the Dow hitting a fresh all-time high as investors welcomed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s remarks at Jackson Hole.

The S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (NYSEARCA:XLV) also gained about 0.85% during the week.

The top S&P 500 healthcare gainers and losers for the last week are as follows:

Top Gainers:

Bio-Techne (TECH) +6.77%

Align Technology (ALGN) +5.22%

Humana (HUM) +4.79%

Zimmer Biomet (ZBH) +4.77%

Molina Healthcare (MOH) +4.57%

Top Losers:

Moderna (MRNA) -3.53%

Gilead Sciences (GILD) -3.16%

Incyte (INCY) -2.04%

Cardinal Health (CAH) -1.37%

Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) -1.35%

Here are some of the important healthcare stories from this week:

Trump backs down from 250% pharma tariff threat

The White House on Thursday released new details about the recently signed U.S.–EU trade deal, indicating that tariffs on European pharmaceutical products will be capped at 15%.

The move contrasts with President Donald Trump’s recent threats to increase pharma tariffs up to 250% amid a so-called Section 232 investigation aimed at evaluating the impact of U.S. drug imports on national security.

Novo Nordisk’s new CEO is said to start hiring freeze to cut costs

Novo Nordisk (NVO) reportedly implemented a global hiring freeze as the Danish drugmaker seeks to cut costs and regain its footing in the competitive market for weight-loss treatments.

The halt will apply across all markets and departments, except business-critical roles, a company spokeswoman told Bloomberg on Wednesday.

Novo’s (NVO) new chief executive, Maziar Mike Doustdar, has signaled that job cuts may be part of a promised revamp, telling reporters last month that Novo needs to exercise greater discipline and prudence on spending.

He said he plans to review the company’s cost base.

Viking Therapeutics plunges after mid-stage trial data for oral weight loss drug

Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) lost ~38% in the premarket on Tuesday after announcing that its oral tablet formulation of its GLP-1/GIP dual receptor agonist VK2735 caused up to 12.2% of weight loss over 13 weeks in a mid-stage trial.

Citing topline data from its Phase 2 VENTURE-Oral Dosing trial, the company noted that the once-daily therapy was found to be safe and well-tolerated throughout the study period, with many of the treatment-emergent adverse events being mild and moderate.

The trial also achieved primary and secondary endpoints with patients on VK2735, experiencing statistically significant weight loss compared to those on placebo.

Additionally, patients on VK2735 witnessed up to a 10.9% reduction of mean body weight relative to placebo, with statistically significant effects.

As for safety, the company noted that 20% of trial participants on VK2735 exited the trial, compared to 13% on placebo.

The placebo-controlled randomized trial evaluated once-daily VK2735 across five dosing groups over 13 weeks in 280 obese or overweight adults who had at least one weight-related condition.

Medtronic lowers tariff impact and raises FY26 earnings outlook

Medtronic (MDT) on Tuesday boosted its full-year earnings outlook to account for better-than-expected financials for Q1 fiscal 2025 and a reduced impact from the Trump administration’s newly imposed tariffs.

Medtronic (MDT), based in Ireland, reiterated its FY26 outlook for organic revenue growth at approximately 5% but raised the diluted non-GAAP earnings guidance to $5.60 - $5.66 per share, up from $5.50 - $5.60 previously, compared to $5.55 in the consensus.

The revised outlook includes about $185M in tariff-related impact compared to the previous projection of $200M-$350M, Medtronic (MDT) said.

Concurrently, the company also announced the appointment of two new independent directors and several other strategic changes as activist investor Elliott Investment Management became one of its largest shareholders.

Novavax downgraded to Underperform at BofA on growth uncertainties

Novavax (NVAX) was downgraded to Underperform from Neutral at Bank of America, citing growth outlook challenges despite acknowledging the company’s strong execution and recent cost-cutting measures.

The research firm argued that the growth outlook for Novavax remains clouded, even as recent negative news around mRNA players, such as BARDA funding cuts, provided a short-term boost to the stock.

According to BofA analysts, the long-term drivers for Novavax are facing mounting uncertainty, making the current share price difficult to justify.

data.title: Top global stories this week: BHP, Softbank-Intel among notable names
data.description:
Most major stock indexes across the globe ended the week higher, as easing US inflation raised hopes of a potential interest rate cut by the Fed at its upcoming meeting. Traders also welcomed an extension of the US–China trade truce.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signalled that the central bank is likely to cut rates at its September meeting, sparking the strongest cross-asset rally since April.

US Composite PMI rose to 55.4 in August, marking the strongest expansion of the year and the 31st straight month of growth, according to flash estimates.

Over the week, S&P 500 (SP500) advanced 0.4% and Dow Jones (DJI) moved up by 1.7%, while the Nasdaq 100 (COMP:IND) fell 0.4%.

Across the Atlantic, the European equities (STOXX) ended the week higher by 1.5% as global sentiment improved. 

Investors assessed the impact of the EU’s new trade deal with the U.S. EU pledged $750B in U.S. energy spending and at least $600B in investment, securing a tariff cap of 15% instead of the threatened 30%. Pharmaceuticals will face 15% levy.

On the data front, the Eurozone PMI hit a 15-month high in August, driven by manufacturing gains and suggesting resilience to global protectionism, bolstering the case for ECB hawks amid rising prices. Meanwhile, FOMC minutes revealed concern over unanchored inflation expectations.

UK private-sector activity grew at the fastest pace in a year in August, with the Composite PMI rising to 53, beating expectations. Inflation also surprised to the upside in July, the highest in 18 months.

In the week, London’s FTSE 100 (UKX) and France (CAC:IND) gained 2% and 1.1% respectively, while Germany (DAX:IND) equities added 0.5%.

Major corporate news from Europe this week:

  • BP’s (BP) operations at its 440,000 bbl/refinery in Whiting, Indiana, were affected by flooding caused by a severe thunderstorm.
  • BHP (BHP) reported its smallest annual underlying profit in five years, as iron ore prices remained under pressure due to oversupply concerns and slowing demand in China.

The Asia-Pacific markets ended the week higher, following record high moves on the Wall Street.

On Monday, China's stock market closed at its highest level in a decade, amid strong buying momentum from investors, and trade tensions with the U.S. seemed to be easing.

Sentiment also improved on hopes of fresh stimulus from Beijing after China’s economy slowed in July. Starting in September, the Chinese government will launch a one-year consumer loan subsidy, covering 90% of interest costs, with local authorities funding the rest.

As expected, the People’s Bank of China held key rates steady in August, keeping the one-year LPR at 3.0% and the five-year at 3.5%.

Chinese markets (SHCOMP) gained 3.1% in the last week.

Meanwhile, Japan’s Nikkei 225 (NKY:IND) lost 2.3% in the week.

Japan's core inflation eased slightly in July but remained above the central bank's target, keeping expectations of another rate hike alive. Meanwhile, manufacturing activity showed signs of improvement in August, edging closer to growth and beating forecasts.

Major Asian corporate news from this week.

  •  Video sharing and gaming platform Bilibili (BILI) reported a top-and-bottom line beat for its second quarter results, helped by strong double-digit percentage gains in its advertising and mobile games businesses.
  • SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) will make a $2B investment in Intel (INTL) common stock, paying $23/share.
  • Sony (SONY) hiked its prices for the PlayStation 5 console by $50 in the U.S., citing a "challenging economic environment."
  • SHEIN is weighing a return to mainland China as it seeks to satisfy regulators and pursue a Hong Kong listing, following unsuccessful IPO attempts in the U.S. and UK.

ETFs to track Europe: ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

ETFs to track Asia & Oceania: (NYSEARCA:EWJ), (NYSE:JEQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:DXJ), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:FXY), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSEARCA:KWEB)

data.title: Earnings week ahead: NVDA, BABA, SNOW, CRWD, OKTA, MRVL, DELL, PDD, LI, BBY, and more
data.description:
Next week marks the final stretch of August earnings, and it’s stacked with market-moving names across AI, tech, retail, financials, and energy.

On the tech front, results from NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA), Alibaba (NYSE:BABA), CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD), Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW), Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL), Okta (NASDAQ:OKTA), Dell (NYSE:DELL), MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB), and Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) will provide a pulse check on AI adoption, cloud spending, and enterprise software demand.

In retail, investors will be watching Best Buy (NYSE:BBY), Dollar General (NYSE:DG), Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ:ULTA), Kohl’s (NYSE:KSS), Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF), The Gap (GAP), DICK’S Sporting Goods (NYSE:DKS), Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN), Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI), and Victoria’s Secret (NYSE:VSCO) for clues on consumer resilience heading into the holiday season.

Financial heavyweights including Toronto-Dominion (NYSE:TD), Bank of Nova Scotia (NYSE:BNS), Royal Bank of Canada (NYSE:RY), Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO), and Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (NYSE:CM) will shed light on credit demand and lending trends.

Meanwhile, Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI), PDD Holdings (NASDAQ:PDD), Trip.com (TCOM), Frontline (FRO), Sibanye Stillwater (SBSW), Daqo New Energy (DQ), Hormel Foods (HRL), and J.M. Smucker (SJM) round out a diverse slate of reports spanning EVs, commodities, energy, and consumer staples.

With NVIDIA’s AI momentum, China’s tech giants, and U.S. retailers all reporting in the same week, markets are set for a busy and potentially volatile close to August.

Below is a rundown of major earnings reports due in the week of August 25 to August 29:

Monday, August 25

Pinduoduo (NASDAQ:PDD)

Chinese e-commerce giant Pinduoduo (PDD) is set to release its Q2 results before the market opens on Monday. Analysts are projecting a significant Y/Y decline in profits, exceeding 37%.

Despite this forecast, PDD's stock has been a standout performer in 2025, with a YTD gain of approximately 27%, far outpacing the broader market.

The company's performance has led to a split in opinion among analysts. While Wall Street maintains a bullish stance with a Buy rating, Seeking Alpha's Quant system has assigned a Hold rating.

SA Investing Group Leader Envision Research also maintains a Buy, noting that while recent trade tensions have increased uncertainty for both JD.com (JD) and PDD, Pinduoduo is better positioned for resilience. Drawing from Peter Lynch’s investment principles, the analysis highlights PDD’s superior profitability, valuation, and operating metrics compared to JD, reinforcing its leadership in China’s fast-growing e-commerce market.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.06
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $14.36B
  • Earnings Insight: Pinduoduo has beaten EPS estimates in 6 of the past 8 quarters, and revenue expectations in 4 of those reports.

Also reporting: HEICO Corporation (HEI), Sasol (SSL), Semtech (SMTC), StealthGas (GASS), and more.

Tuesday, August 26

Okta (NASDAQ:OKTA)

Okta (OKTA) is scheduled to report its Q2 results after the market closes on Tuesday, with analysts forecasting robust performance. Expectations are for a 17% Y/Y increase in profits and more than 10% growth in sales.

Just days ahead of earnings, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating upgraded Okta to Buy from Hold, aligning with Wall Street analysts who also maintain a Buy rating.

According to a Seeking Alpha contributor, Star Investments, Okta's valuation is particularly appealing. The company's price-to-free cash flow (P/FCF) multiple suggests a potential 27% upside from its July 31 closing price. This positive outlook is supported by Okta's consistent market leadership, as evidenced by its eighth consecutive year as a "Leader" in Gartner's 2024 Magic Quadrant. The company is also actively pursuing four key growth initiatives to ensure sustained revenue expansion over the medium and long term.

Another author, Roman Luzgin, highlights additional factors that make Okta a compelling investment. He notes that the company's financial stability, low debt, and reasonable valuation ratios limit downside risk and create a favorable risk/reward profile. Furthermore, he suggests that recent acquisition rumors and sector-wide M&A trends could drive a significant premium, offering substantial speculative upside for Okta shareholders.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.85
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $711.84M
  • Earnings Insight: The company has consecutively outperformed EPS and revenue expectations in straight 8 quarters.

Also reporting: Bank of Nova Scotia (NYSE:BNS), MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB), Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO), Box (BOX), Daqo New Energy (DQ), EHang Holdings Limited (EH), and more.

Wednesday, August 27

NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA)

Nvidia (NVDA) is set to be the centerpiece of this week's earnings calendar, with its Q2 report scheduled for release after the market closes on Wednesday.

Analysts are forecasting ~50% Y/Y growth in both sales and profits, fueled by relentless demand for AI chips and accelerating data center momentum.

While Wall Street analysts maintain a Strong Buy consensus, Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains more cautious, rating NVDA a Hold on valuation concerns.

Adding to skepticism, SA contributor Bears of Wall Street reiterates a Sell, warning that Nvidia’s renewed ability to sell H20 AI accelerators in China may provide only limited upside, given its overexposure to a handful of major customers and rising risk as hyperscale’s develop in-house AI chips.

Similarly, SA author Hawkinvest maintains a Strong Sell, noting that while Nvidia is an exceptional company, its stock is “priced for perfection.” With NVDA trading at 40x earnings and 20x revenues, competition intensifying, and the stock now comprising 8% of the S&P 500, making it vulnerable to a pullback, particularly ahead of the earnings report.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.01
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $45.97B
  • Earnings Insight: Nvidia outperformed revenue and EPS estimates all the last 8 quarters.

Also reporting: CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD), Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW), HP (HPQ), Royal Bank of Canada (NYSE:RY), Veeva Systems (VEEV), Agilent Technologies (A), Kohl's (NYSE:KSS), Nutanix (NTNX), NetApp (NTAP), Pure Storage (PSTG), J.M. Smucker (SJM), and more.

Thursday, August 28

Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL)

Dell Technologies (DELL) is set to deliver its Q2 print on Thursday after market hours. Analysts expect a strong result with profit growth of more than 20% Y/Y on sales growth of near 17%.

The stock is up about 11% in 2025, and sentiment remains broadly bullish. Both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system assign it a Buy.

SA Investing Group Leader Danil Sereda remains bullish on Dell (DELL), noting the stock has doubled since his April call but still has strong AI server and PC refresh drivers. He sees Q2 earnings and potential guidance upgrades as catalysts, while partnerships with Nvidia and Elastic support a shift toward higher-margin, software-like revenue. Despite risks around AI server margins, he maintains a Buy rating with 22% upside potential.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.29
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $29.19B
  • Earnings Insight: DELL has beaten EPS estimates in 6 of the past 8 quarters, and revenue expectations in 5 of those reports.

Also reporting: Marvell Technology Group (NASDAQ:MRVL), TD Bank Group (NYSE:TD), Li Auto (NASDAQ:LI), Best Buy (NYSE:BBY), Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK), Affirm Holdings (AFRM), ULTA Beauty (NASDAQ:ULTA), Dollar General Corporation (NYSE:DG), Hormel Foods (HRL), Ambarella (AMBA), Elastic N.V. (ESTC), Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI), Baozun (BZUN), Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (NYSE:CM), Victoria's Secret & Co. (NYSE:VSCO), DICK'S Sporting Goods (NYSE:DKS), SentinelOne (S), and more.

Friday, August 29

Alibaba (NYSE:BABA)

Alibaba (BABA) is set to report its FQ1 earnings on Friday, a report that will be a key indicator for China's consumer recovery and the broader sentiment toward Chinese tech stocks. Analyst consensus points to approximately 4% Y/Y revenue growth but a 6% decline in profits, highlighting the mixed outlook.

Wall Street analysts remain bullish, assigning Alibaba a Strong Buy rating, driven by expectations of improved efficiency, expanding cloud margins, and steady e-commerce demand. However, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system maintains a Hold, reflecting lingering concerns around growth sustainability and macroeconomic risks.

Envision Research, a Seeking Alpha Investing Group leader, also holds a neutral stance. The firm points to heightened systemic and geopolitical risks, citing the notable exit of Bridgewater Associates from US-listed Chinese stocks, including BABA. This factor, along with the suspension of the "De minimis" exemption, which poses threats to Alibaba's cross-border e-commerce business, is seen as largely offsetting the appeal of the company's low P/E and attractive PEG ratios. On a positive note, Envision Research does see promise for accelerated demand within Alibaba's cloud and AI segments.

In contrast, Andres Cardenal, another Seeking Alpha Investing Group leader, presents a Strong Buy argument. He contends that while Alibaba's decade-long underperformance underscores the risks of Chinese stocks, the current negative sentiment creates a compelling contrarian opportunity.

Cardenal highlights the company's aggressive "all-in AI" pivot, its leadership in open-source models, and its cloud growth, which position it as a top AI player in China. Despite competitive pressures, he notes that Alibaba remains highly profitable with strong financials and a valuation significantly below its global AI peers, suggesting an asymmetric upside, though active risk management is essential.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $2.15
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $35.32B
  • Earnings Insight: The company has beaten EPS estimates in 5 of the past 8 quarters, and revenue estimates in 4 of those reports.
Also reporting: BRP (DOOO), Frontline (FRO), Lanvin Group (LANV), and more.
